words.definitionEn: involving many people or things, especially over a wide area
words.exampleEn: Large areas of the forest will be cleared for ranching as part of a large-scale development plan.

words.definitionEn: a device that gives out light in which all the waves oscillate (= change direction and strength) together, typically producing a powerful beam of light that can be used for cutting metal, in medical operations, etc.
words.exampleEn: a laser beam

words.definitionEn: relating to architecture and buildings
words.exampleEn: architectural features

words.definitionEn: used to refer to the second of two things or people mentioned
words.exampleEn: He chose the latter option.

words.definitionEn: the second of two things or people mentioned
words.exampleEn: He presented two solutions. The latter seems much better.

words.definitionEn: an area of ground covered in short grass in a garden or park, or used for playing a game on
words.exampleEn: In summer we have to mow the lawn twice a week.

words.definitionEn: a claim or complaint against somebody that a person or an organization can make in court
words.exampleEn: He filed a lawsuit against his record label.

words.definitionEn: the way in which the parts of something such as the page of a book, a website, a garden or a building are arranged
words.exampleEn: the layout of streets

words.definitionEn: a collection of historical documents or records of a government, a family, a place or an organization; the place where these records are stored
words.exampleEn: the National Sound Archive

words.definitionEn: a small hole that lets liquid or gas flow in or out of something by accident
words.exampleEn: a leak in the roof

words.definitionEn: to allow liquid or gas to get in or out through a small hole
words.exampleEn: a leaking pipe

words.definitionEn: a long or high jump
words.exampleEn: a leap of six metres

words.definitionEn: to jump high or a long way
words.exampleEn: + adv./prep., A dolphin leapt out of the water.

words.definitionEn: a place with a flat open area in the middle and seats around it where people can watch sports and entertainment
words.exampleEn: a concert at Wembley Arena

words.definitionEn: money or property that is given to you by somebody when they die
words.exampleEn: They each received a legacy of $5 000.

words.definitionEn: very famous and talked about a lot by people
words.exampleEn: a legendary figure

words.definitionEn: a law or a set of laws passed by a parliament
words.exampleEn: an important piece of legislation

words.definitionEn: connected with the act of making and passing laws
words.exampleEn: a legislative assembly/body/council

words.definitionEn: a group of people who have the power to make and change laws
words.exampleEn: a democratically elected legislature

words.definitionEn: used (often before a comparative or superlative adjective) when you are stating an opinion that you believe you could give reasons to support
words.exampleEn: He is arguably the best actor of his generation.

words.definitionEn: for which there is a fair and acceptable reason
words.exampleEn: a legitimate grievance

words.definitionEn: very long, and often too long, in time or size
words.exampleEn: lengthy delays

words.definitionEn: sexually attracted to other women; connected with lesbians
words.exampleEn: the lesbian and gay community

words.definitionEn: not as great in size, amount or importance as something/somebody else
words.exampleEn: people of lesser importance

words.definitionEn: causing or able to cause death
words.exampleEn: She had been given a lethal dose of poison.

words.definitionEn: legally responsible for paying the cost of something
words.exampleEn: You will be liable for any damage caused.

words.definitionEn: willing to understand and respect other people’s behaviour, opinions, etc., especially when they are different from your own; believing people should be able to choose how they behave
words.exampleEn: liberal attitudes/views/opinions

words.definitionEn: a person who understands and respects other people’s opinions and behaviour, especially when they are different from their own
words.exampleEn: He liked to think of himself as a liberal.

words.definitionEn: the act or process of freeing a country or a person from the control of somebody else
words.exampleEn: a war of liberation

words.definitionEn: freedom to live as you choose without too many limits from government or authority
words.exampleEn: the fight for justice and liberty

words.definitionEn: to give somebody official permission to do, own, or use something
words.exampleEn: license something, The new drug has not yet been licensed in the US.

words.definitionEn: lasting or existing all through your life
words.exampleEn: Paul became his lifelong friend.

words.definitionEn: the chance of something happening; how likely something is to happen
words.exampleEn: There is very little likelihood of that happening.

words.definitionEn: an arm or a leg; a similar part of an animal, such as a wing
words.exampleEn: an artificial limb

words.definitionEn: to provide weapons for yourself/somebody in order to fight a battle or a war
words.exampleEn: The country was arming against the enemy.

words.definitionEn: the people who are going to take part in a particular event
words.exampleEn: an impressive line-up of speakers

words.definitionEn: of or in lines
words.exampleEn: In his art he broke the laws of scientific linear perspective.

words.definitionEn: to continue to exist for longer than expected
words.exampleEn: The faint smell of her perfume lingered in the room.

words.definitionEn: a list, especially an official or published list of people or things, often arranged in alphabetical order
words.exampleEn: a comprehensive listing of all airlines

words.definitionEn: the ability to read and write
words.exampleEn: a campaign to promote adult literacy

words.definitionEn: a large organ in the body that cleans the blood and produces bile
words.exampleEn: liver disease

words.definitionEn: a large area inside the entrance of a public building where people can meet and wait
words.exampleEn: a hotel lobby

words.definitionEn: to try to influence a politician or the government and, for example, persuade them to support or oppose a change in the law
words.exampleEn: Farmers will lobby Congress for higher subsidies.

words.definitionEn: that can be reached, entered, used, seen, etc.
words.exampleEn: The remote desert area is accessible only by helicopter.

words.definitionEn: a thick piece of wood that is cut from or has fallen from a tree
words.exampleEn: logs for the fire

words.definitionEn: to put information in an official record or write a record of events
words.exampleEn: The police log all phone calls.

words.definitionEn: a way of thinking or explaining something
words.exampleEn: I fail to see the logic behind his argument.

words.definitionEn: that has existed or lasted for a long time
words.exampleEn: a long-standing relationship

words.definitionEn: having been the particular thing mentioned for a long time
words.exampleEn: his long-time colleague

words.definitionEn: to appear as a large shape that is not clear, especially in a frightening way
words.exampleEn: A dark shape loomed up ahead of us.

words.definitionEn: a shape like a curve or circle made by a line curving right round
words.exampleEn: The road went in a huge loop around the lake.

words.definitionEn: a group or collection of things or people, often one that is large or impressive
words.exampleEn: a vast array of bottles of different shapes and sizes

words.definitionEn: the quality of being constant in your support of somebody/something
words.exampleEn: Can I count on your loyalty?

words.definitionEn: machines as a group, especially large ones
words.exampleEn: agricultural/industrial machinery

words.definitionEn: containing magic; used in magic
words.exampleEn: magical powers

words.definitionEn: an official who acts as a judge in the lowest courts of law
words.exampleEn: to come up before the magistrates

words.definitionEn: behaving like a magnet; that can be attracted by a magnet
words.exampleEn: magnetic materials

words.definitionEn: the great size or importance of something; the degree to which something is large or important
words.exampleEn: We did not realize the magnitude of the problem.

words.definitionEn: the main area of land of a country or region, not including any islands near to it
words.exampleEn: to/from the mainland, a boat to/from the mainland

words.definitionEn: considered normal because it reflects what is done or accepted by most people
words.exampleEn: mainstream culture/politics

words.definitionEn: the ideas and opinions that are thought to be normal because they are shared by most people; the people whose ideas and opinions are most accepted
words.exampleEn: His radical views place him outside the mainstream of American politics.

words.definitionEn: the act of keeping something in good condition by checking or repairing it regularly
words.exampleEn: The school pays for heating and the maintenance of the buildings.

words.definitionEn: the authority to do something, given to a government or other organization by the people who vote for it in an election
words.exampleEn: It is undemocratic to govern an area without an electoral mandate.

words.definitionEn: required by law
words.exampleEn: The offence carries a mandatory life sentence.

words.definitionEn: to show something clearly, especially a feeling, an attitude or a quality
words.exampleEn: Social tensions were manifested in the recent political crisis.

words.definitionEn: to control or influence somebody/something, often in a dishonest way so that they do not realize it
words.exampleEn: manipulate somebody/something, She uses her charm to manipulate people.

words.definitionEn: behaviour that controls or influences somebody/something, often in a dishonest way so that they do not realize it
words.exampleEn: Advertising like this is a cynical manipulation of the elderly.

words.definitionEn: a copy of a book, piece of music, etc. before it has been printed
words.exampleEn: an unpublished/original manuscript

words.definitionEn: an organized walk by many people from one place to another, in order to protest about something, or to express their opinions
words.exampleEn: protest marches

words.definitionEn: to walk with stiff regular steps like a soldier
words.exampleEn: Quick march! (= the order to start marching)

words.definitionEn: small and not important
words.exampleEn: a marginal improvement in weather conditions

words.definitionEn: connected with the sea and the creatures and plants that live there
words.exampleEn: marine life

words.definitionEn: the activity of competing with other companies to buy and sell goods, services, etc.
words.exampleEn: Companies must be able to survive in the marketplace.

words.definitionEn: something that you wear over your face to hide it, or to frighten or entertain other people
words.exampleEn: The robbers wore stocking masks.

words.definitionEn: the killing of a large number of people especially in a cruel way
words.exampleEn: the bloody massacre of innocent civilians

words.definitionEn: to express or explain your thoughts or feelings clearly in words
words.exampleEn: She struggled to articulate her thoughts.

words.definitionEn: connected with or involving mathematics
words.exampleEn: mathematical calculations/problems/models

words.definitionEn: behaving in a sensible way, like an adult
words.exampleEn: Jane is very mature for her age.

words.definitionEn: to become fully grown or developed
words.exampleEn: This particular breed of cattle matures early.

words.definitionEn: to increase something as much as possible
words.exampleEn: to maximize efficiency/fitness/profits

words.definitionEn: serious and important
words.exampleEn: a meaningful relationship/discussion/experience

words.definitionEn: for a short period of time but not permanently
words.exampleEn: I'm changing my email address but for the meantime you can use the old one.

words.definitionEn: connected with the Middle Ages (about AD 1000 to AD 1450)
words.exampleEn: medieval architecture/castles/manuscripts

words.definitionEn: the practice of focusing your mind in silence, especially for religious reasons or in order to make your mind calm
words.exampleEn: She found peace through yoga and meditation.

words.definitionEn: a tune, especially the main tune in a piece of music written for several instruments or voices
words.exampleEn: a haunting melody

words.definitionEn: an official note from one person to another in the same organization
words.exampleEn: to write/send a memo

words.definitionEn: an account written by somebody, especially somebody famous, about their life and experiences
words.exampleEn: O'Connor published a childhood memoir.

words.definitionEn: a statue, stone, etc. that is built in order to remind people of an important past event or of a famous person who has died
words.exampleEn: a war memorial (= in memory of soldiers who died in a war)

words.definitionEn: an experienced person who advises and helps somebody with less experience over a period of time
words.exampleEn: She was a friend and mentor to many young actors.

words.definitionEn: a person who buys and sells goods in large quantities, especially one who imports and exports goods
words.exampleEn: a coal/wine merchant

words.definitionEn: a kind or forgiving attitude towards somebody that you have the power to harm or right to punish
words.exampleEn: to ask/beg/plead for mercy

words.definitionEn: used when you want to emphasize how small, unimportant, etc. somebody/something is
words.exampleEn: It took her a mere 20 minutes to win.

words.definitionEn: used meaning ‘only’ or ‘simply’ to emphasize a fact or something that you are saying
words.exampleEn: It is not merely a job, but a way of life.

words.definitionEn: to combine or make two or more things combine to form a single thing
words.exampleEn: The banks are set to merge next year.

words.definitionEn: the act of joining two or more organizations or businesses into one
words.exampleEn: If the merger goes through, thousands of jobs will be lost.

words.definitionEn: the quality of being good and of deserving praise or reward
words.exampleEn: a work of outstanding artistic merit

words.definitionEn: a set of methods and principles used to perform a particular activity
words.exampleEn: recent changes in the methodology of language teaching

words.definitionEn: the grey or black powder that is left after something, especially tobacco, wood or coal, has burnt
words.exampleEn: cigarette ash

words.definitionEn: the middle part of something
words.exampleEn: in the midst of something, Such beauty was unexpected in the midst of the city.

words.definitionEn: the movement every year of large numbers of birds or animals from one place to another
words.exampleEn: the seasonal migration of blue whales

words.definitionEn: using, or willing to use, force or strong pressure to achieve your aims, especially to achieve social or political change
words.exampleEn: militant groups/leaders

words.definitionEn: a person who uses, or is willing to use, force or strong pressure to achieve their aims, especially to achieve social or political change
words.exampleEn: Student militants were fighting with the police.

words.definitionEn: a group of people who are not professional soldiers but who have had military training and can act as an army
words.exampleEn: He said he would call out the state militia if the rebels did not surrender.

words.definitionEn: a building fitted with equipment for grinding grain into flour; a machine for grinding grain
words.exampleEn: The old mill has been converted into apartments.

words.definitionEn: very small in size or amount; as small as possible
words.exampleEn: The work was carried out at minimal cost.

words.definitionEn: to reduce something, especially something bad, to the lowest possible level
words.exampleEn: Good hygiene helps to minimize the risk of infection.

words.definitionEn: the process of getting coal and other minerals from under the ground; the industry involved in this
words.exampleEn: coal/diamond/gold/tin mining

words.definitionEn: a government department that has a particular area of responsibility
words.exampleEn: The Ministry of Defence has issued the following statement.

words.definitionEn: extremely small
words.exampleEn: minute amounts of chemicals in the water

words.definitionEn: an act or event that does not follow the laws of nature and is believed to be caused by God
words.exampleEn: the miracle of rising from the grave

words.definitionEn: great physical or mental pain
words.exampleEn: Fame brought her nothing but misery.

words.definitionEn: giving the wrong idea or impression and making you believe something that is not true
words.exampleEn: misleading information/advertisements

words.definitionEn: a weapon that is sent through the air and that explodes when it hits the thing that it is aimed at
words.exampleEn: nuclear missiles

words.definitionEn: a large crowd of people, especially one that may become violent or cause trouble
words.exampleEn: an angry/unruly mob

words.definitionEn: the ability to move easily from one place, social class or job to another
words.exampleEn: social/geographical/career mobility

words.definitionEn: to work together in order to achieve a particular aim; to organize a group of people to do this
words.exampleEn: The unions mobilized thousands of workers in a protest against the cuts.

words.definitionEn: that is neither very good, large, hot, etc. nor very bad, small, cold, etc.
words.exampleEn: students of moderate ability

words.definitionEn: the act or process of changing something in order to improve it or make it more acceptable; a change that is made
words.exampleEn: Considerable modification of the existing system is needed.

words.definitionEn: the ability to keep increasing or developing
words.exampleEn: The fight for his release gathers momentum each day.

words.definitionEn: a strong desire to have or do something
words.exampleEn: I didn't realize you had political aspirations.

words.definitionEn: a member of a religious group of men who often live apart from other people in a monastery and who do not marry or have personal possessions
words.exampleEn: Benedictine/Buddhist monks

words.definitionEn: the complete control of trade in particular goods or the supply of a particular service; a type of goods or a service that is controlled in this way
words.exampleEn: In the past central government had a monopoly on television broadcasting.

words.definitionEn: to have a strong desire to achieve or to become something
words.exampleEn: aspire (to something), She aspired to a scientific career.

words.definitionEn: principles relating to right and wrong or good and bad behaviour
words.exampleEn: matters of public/private morality

words.definitionEn: the murder of an important or famous person, especially for political reasons
words.exampleEn: The president survived a number of assassination attempts.

words.definitionEn: the crime of attacking somebody physically
words.exampleEn: Both men were charged with assault.

words.definitionEn: a reason for doing something
words.exampleEn: There seemed to be no motive for the murder.

words.definitionEn: a person driving a car
words.exampleEn: The accident was reported by a passing motorist.

words.definitionEn: to attack somebody violently, especially when this is a crime
words.exampleEn: He has been charged with assaulting a police officer.

words.definitionEn: to come together as a group; to bring people or things together as a group
words.exampleEn: All the students were asked to assemble in the main hall.

words.definitionEn: connected with or belonging to a town, city or district that has its own local government
words.exampleEn: municipal elections/councils

words.definitionEn: a group of people who have been elected to meet together regularly and make decisions or laws for a particular region or country
words.exampleEn: state/legislative/federal/local assemblies

words.definitionEn: used to describe feelings that two or more people have for each other equally, or actions that affect two or more people equally
words.exampleEn: mutual respect/understanding

words.definitionEn: to state clearly and definitely that something is true
words.exampleEn: assert that…, She continued to assert that she was innocent.

words.definitionEn: used to introduce more exact and detailed information about something that you have just mentioned
words.exampleEn: We need to concentrate on our target audience, namely women aged between 20 and 30.
